documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: PoC command injection in BLE service of Norton Core Secure WiFi Router (CVE-2018-5234).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: PoC command injection in BLE service of Norton Core Secure WiFi Router (CVE-2018-5234).
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: pip install -r ./requirements.txt. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./ble_norton_core.py "/etc/init.d/dropbear start"`. Further invocations from upstream: `ssh root@norton.core`. PoC command injection in BLE service of Norton Core Secure WiFi Router (CVE-2018-5234).

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Bluetooth, use ble_norton_core because PoC command injection in BLE service of Norton Core Secure WiFi Router (CVE-2018-5234).
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./ble_norton_core.py "/etc/init.d/dropbear start"` as documented upstream for ble_norton_core.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `ssh root@norton.core` as documented upstream for ble_norton_core.
When chaining Bluetooth tools, pair ble_norton_core with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ble_norton_core --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
